thesis: Adaptive Biotechnologies is the leader in minimal residual disease (MRD) testing for blood cancers via its FDA-cleared clonoSEQ assay, which grew 46% in FY2025 and now drives 95% of revenue. The company has guided to profitability in 2026, but the stock has already rallied 80%+ over the past year and RSI sits at 71 (overbought). Insider selling of $16M in recent months adds caution. A compelling long-term story, but better entry on a pullback to $16.

drivers: MRD test volume grows 50%+ in 2026 with expanding indications
Immune Medicine lands a major pharma partnership
Company achieves profitability in 2026, re-rating from pre-profitability biotech to high-growth diagnostics

risks: Test volume growth decelerates as market matures
Payer pushback or competitive pressure from Natera Signatera erodes pricing
Cash burn forces dilutive raise below $15

base_case.assumptions: MRD grows 35–40% with stable pricing; Immune Medicine stabilizes; breakeven achieved in H1 2027.

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — test volume trends critical
Q3 2026 guidance update on profitability timeline

key_risks: Competition from Natera Signatera and other MRD assays
Funding risk if profitability is delayed beyond 2026
